cases.title: Signup, login, and GET /me/registrations succeed end-to-end via API
preconditions: API server running at /api/v1
Published event in RegistrationOpen state with available capacity
Fresh unique email not yet registered in the system
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with { email, password, displayName } for a new participant
POST /api/v1/auth/login with the same email and password
Use returned JWT as Authorization bearer on POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations to create one registration
GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20 with the login JWT
cases.expected: Register returns JWT and profile with Participant role; login returns fresh JWT and profile; GET /me/registrations returns HTTP 200 with paginated envelope containing the participant's registration (registrationId, eventId, eventName, state, updatedAt), total ≥ 1, and valid page metadata

cases.title: Browser signup → login → My Registrations page shows own registrations
preconditions: Web app running with /signup, /login, and protected My Registrations route
Published open event available for registration
Fresh browser context with no stored JWT
steps: Navigate to /signup and submit email, password, and display name
Sign out or clear session if auto-signed-in, then navigate to /login and sign in with the same credentials
Register for an open event via participant UI
Navigate to My Registrations (or equivalent protected route)
Observe TopBar account button and registration list
cases.expected: Signup and login succeed; TopBar shows participant displayName; My Registrations page renders the user's registration with correct event name and status; no redirect to /login after authenticated navigation

cases.title: Self-service signup assigns Participant role and login JWT sub matches users.id
preconditions: Auth module and users table available
No existing user with target email
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with valid credentials
Decode returned JWT and read sub claim
Query users table for the new email
POST /api/v1/auth/login with same credentials and decode JWT sub
cases.expected: New user row exists with Participant role only (not OrganizerAdmin or OrganizerStaff); register and login JWT sub equals users.id UUID; GET /me with login token returns matching profile id and roles array containing Participant

cases.title: Authenticated participant retrieves paginated own registrations with correct envelope
preconditions: Participant account with at least two registrations across events (Registered and/or Waitlisted states)
Valid participant JWT from POST /auth/login
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20 with bearer token
Verify each item belongs to authenticated participant
GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=1 and confirm total and totalPages consistency
cases.expected: Response matches paginated envelope (items, page, pageSize, total, totalPages); every item's participantId or implicit actor scope matches JWT sub; default sort updatedAt:desc applied; pageSize boundary respected per pagination contract

cases.title: Post-login redirect resumes intended My Registrations route after signup/login gate
preconditions: Route guard preserves return URL when unauthenticated user hits protected route
Fresh unregistered email available
steps: While unauthenticated, navigate directly to My Registrations protected route
Complete signup on /signup or follow redirect to /login and sign in with new account
Observe post-auth navigation
cases.expected: After successful login, user lands on originally requested My Registrations route (or equivalent); page loads registration list without second manual navigation; satisfies signup → login → view own registrations UX per Flow 0 and AC-16

cases.title: GET /me/registrations returns only current actor's registrations, never cross-participant data
preconditions: Two distinct participant accounts each with registrations on different events
Valid JWT for participant A from login
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations as participant A
Compare returned registrationIds against all registrations in database for participant B
cases.expected: Response items contain only participant A's registrations; no registrationId, eventId, or status row belonging to participant B appears; scope constraint per roles-permissions §3 enforced at query layer

cases.title: Signup persists password with adaptive hashing, not plaintext
preconditions: Database access to users credential storage column
Fresh signup email
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with known password value
Read stored password hash from users table for the new account
cases.expected: Stored value is bcrypt or argon2 hash (recognizable prefix and length); plaintext password is not persisted; login still succeeds with original password via POST /auth/login

cases.title: My registrations list requires authenticated session per NFR-07
preconditions: Participant with existing registrations
Ability to call GET /me/registrations with and without Authorization header
steps: Complete signup and login flow for participant
GET /api/v1/me/registrations with valid bearer token
Repeat GET /api/v1/me/registrations without Authorization header
cases.expected: Authenticated request returns 200 with items; unauthenticated request returns 401; sensitive self-service read is gated behind authentication per NFR-07

cases.title: Newly signed-up user with zero registrations receives empty paginated list
preconditions: Fresh participant account created via POST /auth/register and authenticated via POST /auth/login
Participant has not registered for any event
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20 with participant JWT
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with items: [], total: 0, totalPages: 0 or 1 per pagination rules, and valid page/pageSize metadata; flow still succeeds end-to-end despite empty list

cases.title: Duplicate signup with same email is rejected
preconditions: Existing participant account with email user@example.com
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with the same email, different password and displayName
cases.expected: HTTP 400 or 409 with stable error code (e.g. duplicate email); no second user row created; subsequent login still works only with original account credentials

cases.title: Login with wrong password after successful signup fails
preconditions: Participant account created via POST /auth/register with known password
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/login with correct email and incorrect password
cases.expected: HTTP 401 or 400 with authentication failure; no JWT returned; GET /me/registrations without valid token remains blocked

cases.title: My registrations page beyond last page returns empty items with valid metadata
preconditions: Participant logged in with exactly one registration (total=1, pageSize=20)
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=99&pageSize=20 with bearer token
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with items: [] and consistent total, totalPages, page, pageSize fields per AC-13 pagination contract; no error for out-of-range page index

cases.title: Re-login after sign-out restores access to My Registrations
preconditions: Participant completed signup, registered for an event, and viewed My Registrations
Client JWT cleared via TopBar sign-out (Flow 0b step 5)
steps: Sign out from TopBar user menu
Navigate to /login and sign in with original credentials
Open My Registrations again
cases.expected: After re-login, protected route accessible without permanent lockout; registration list repopulates with same user's data; signup → login → view flow repeatable across sessions

cases.title: Signup with invalid input rejects before account creation
preconditions: No existing user with target email
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with missing password or invalid email format
POST /api/v1/auth/register with password below minimum policy length if enforced
cases.expected: HTTP 400 with INVALID_INPUT or equivalent stable code; no user row persisted; login with submitted credentials fails

cases.title: My registrations query uses single paginated fetch without N+1 fan-out
preconditions: Participant with multiple registrations across events
Database query logging or integration test spy enabled
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20 via service layer or HTTP
Count database round-trips for list query and event name resolution
cases.expected: List endpoint resolves eventName (or equivalent display fields) without per-row N+1 queries; aligns with testing-plan scenario matrix expectation for my registrations paginated fetch
